meta.title: Spec stronger markdown crawling
meta.summary: Docs-only PR (3 new files, +283/-0). It specs a redesign of Hubble Desktop markdown crawling so the editor shell stays usable while the sidebar builds an ignore-aware, incrementally-updated markdown index. No code changes yet; the views map the existing desktop file subsystem and the behavior/architecture the specs propose.

graphs.label: System overview
graphs.summary: How Hubble Desktop's file/sidebar subsystem is wired today: disk access lives in Electron main, reaches the renderer over a typed bridge, lands in a small store, and renders in the sidebar.

nodes.title: Electron main process
nodes.summary: The only layer that touches disk. apps/desktop/electron/main.ts holds a path-grant allowlist, registers IPC handlers (desktop:list-directory recursively crawls markdown, desktop:watch-path runs chokidar), parses layered .gitignore/.ignore via the `ignore` package, and always hard-ignores .git, dist, and node_modules.
details: collectMarkdownFiles() walks directories depth-first, applies inherited ignore rules per directory, and emits FileEntry { path, modified_at } for .md/.markdown/.mdown only.
A chokidar watcher per watchId streams add/change/unlink/addDir/unlinkDir events; editing a .gitignore/.ignore replaces the watcher so ignore scope stays current.
Grants (granted files + roots) gate every path so the renderer can only reach explicitly allowed locations.

nodes.title: Preload bridge (desktopApi)
nodes.summary: apps/desktop/electron/preload.ts exposes a context-isolated, typed DesktopApi over ipcRenderer. The renderer never touches Node fs directly; listDirectory, readFileText, the pickers, and watchPath are all bridge calls. watchPath allocates a numeric watchId and turns main-process file events into a renderer subscription with an unsubscribe handle.
details: The DesktopApi surface is typed in apps/desktop/src/desktopApi/types.ts, keeping renderer and main in sync.
watchPath(path, { recursive }, callback) returns an Unsubscribe; recursive watches a folder, non-recursive watches a single file.

nodes.title: Renderer store
nodes.summary: A @simplestack/store appStore split into workspaceStore (workspacePath, files, recentWorkspaces, lastOpenedPaths, sortMode) and viewerStore (currentPath, content, external-change status). apps/desktop/src/store/actions.ts owns refreshFiles, openWorkspace, loadPath, and file mutations; persistence.ts saves everything except the file list, so the list is rebuilt each launch.
details: refreshFiles(path) calls listDirectory and replaces workspace.files, guarding against a stale workspacePath.
isInWorkspace / lastOpenedPaths track which file to restore per workspace; touchFile bumps mtime on local saves.

nodes.title: Sidebar + App watchers
nodes.summary: apps/desktop/src/components/Sidebar.tsx adapts workspace.files into the shared @hubble.md/ui sidebar (relative display paths, sort modes, select→loadPath, rename/delete). apps/desktop/src/App.tsx runs two watcher effects: a recursive workspace watcher and a single-file active-document watcher that feeds external-change conflict handling.
details: The workspace watcher effect currently reacts to any change by re-listing the whole folder.
The active-file watcher re-reads the open document and classifies match/reload/conflict against the editor baseline.

tour.title: Start where disk access lives
tour.body: Electron main is the single owner of filesystem access: grants, recursive markdown crawling, ignore parsing, and chokidar watchers all live here. Everything else is downstream of this layer.

tour.title: Cross the typed bridge
tour.body: The renderer reaches main only through the context-isolated desktopApi. Knowing this bridge exists explains why file reads, lists, and watches are all async IPC calls rather than direct fs access.

tour.title: Land in the store
tour.body: Bridge results populate a small @simplestack store split into workspace and document state. Note the file list is intentionally not persisted, so it is rebuilt on each launch.

tour.title: Render and watch
tour.body: The sidebar renders workspace.files through the shared UI, while App.tsx wires the folder and active-file watchers. This is the surface a reviewer will see behavior changes in.

graphs.label: Data flow graph
graphs.summary: How markdown file state moves from intent through crawling and watcher deltas into renderer state and the sidebar. The specs propose owning incremental index state instead of re-listing on every event.

nodes.title: Indexing intent (specs)
nodes.summary: The specs define the target: keep the shell usable, show a sidebar indexing state, and update incrementally while respecting ignore files.
details: PRODUCT.md sets the user-visible contract; TECH.md sets the module plan; ADR-0008 frames the sidebar index as ephemeral navigation state, not an access boundary.
No existing PR review comments were found at the head commit.

nodes.title: Initial crawl + ignore rules
nodes.summary: A recursive walk produces the markdown FileEntry[] for the open folder, filtering by layered ignore rules and hard-ignored dirs.
details: Today collectMarkdownFiles() in main.ts emits the snapshot via desktop:list-directory.
TECH.md proposes moving this into fileIndex.ts + ignoreRules.ts that own crawl state and emit an initial 'ready' snapshot.
Crawl includes .md/.markdown/.mdown and always excludes .git, dist, node_modules.

nodes.title: Chokidar watcher deltas
nodes.summary: The folder watcher emits add/change/unlink/dir events; ignore-config edits rebuild rules and replace the watcher.
details: desktop:watch-path filters to markdown and ignore-config files, then notifies the renderer.
TECH.md proposes batching these into typed index deltas (upsert/remove) with a short debounce instead of a full re-list.

nodes.title: workspace.files state
nodes.summary: The renderer store holds the file list the sidebar renders. Today every watcher event triggers refreshFiles(); the spec replaces that with delta application.
details: refreshFiles() sets workspace.files (guarded by workspacePath); touchFile() bumps mtime on save.
TECH.md adds a fileIndexStatus (idle/indexing/ready/error) and upsert/remove reducers so live edits avoid full re-crawls.

nodes.title: Sidebar render
nodes.summary: The sidebar renders the current file list. The spec adds a dedicated indexing state and allows the open file to be absent from the list.
details: Sidebar.tsx maps workspace.files into the shared @hubble.md/ui sidebar with relative paths and sort modes.
PRODUCT.md requires an indexing/loading state during the first crawl and a complete tree afterward.

tour.title: Start with intent
tour.body: Begin with what the specs are trying to achieve: a usable shell plus an ignore-aware, incremental sidebar index. This is the source of every flow below.

tour.title: Initial crawl
tour.body: The first data to move is the initial markdown snapshot from a recursive, ignore-filtered crawl. The spec relocates this into a dedicated indexer module.

tour.title: Live deltas
tour.body: After the snapshot, the chokidar watcher produces incremental file events. The spec batches these into typed upsert/remove deltas instead of full re-lists.

tour.title: Into renderer state
tour.body: Both the snapshot and deltas land in workspace.files. The key spec change is applying deltas and an index status here rather than calling refreshFiles() on every event.

tour.title: Out to the sidebar
tour.body: Finally the store renders into the sidebar, which gains an explicit indexing state and tolerates the open file being absent from the list.

graphs.label: Code dependency graph
graphs.summary: Which modules the specs touch and how they depend on each other: specs guide renderer entry points, which depend on the store and the typed bridge, which invokes the main-process crawler that the spec splits into an indexer.

nodes.title: specs/gh-31 + ADR-0008
nodes.summary: The only files actually added by this PR. They define intent (PRODUCT), the module plan (TECH), and the architectural decision (ADR).
details: PRODUCT.md: 28 numbered behaviors + a UX validation script.
TECH.md: proposed fileIndex.ts/ignoreRules.ts modules and renderer/IPC changes.
ADR-0008: sidebar index is ephemeral navigation state, refining ADR-0001.

nodes.title: App.tsx watchers
nodes.summary: Renderer entry that wires workspace + active-file watchers and startup path restoration. Spec target: subscribe to index updates instead of re-listing.
details: Today the workspace watcher effect calls refreshFiles(workspacePath) on every change.
TECH.md: subscribe to a workspace-index subscription and keep startup restoration independent of index readiness.

nodes.title: store/actions.ts
nodes.summary: Owns refreshFiles, openWorkspace, loadPath and file mutations. Spec target: add delta reducers and decouple loadPath from indexing.
details: openWorkspace currently awaits refreshFiles before loading the remembered file.
TECH.md: return without awaiting the crawl, apply upsert/remove deltas, keep full refresh only as a fallback.

nodes.title: preload + desktopApi types
nodes.summary: The typed IPC contract between renderer and main. Spec target: add a watchWorkspaceFiles-style index subscription.
details: types.ts defines DesktopApi; preload.ts implements watchPath/listDirectory over ipcRenderer.
TECH.md: add a workspace-index subscription that sends { status, snapshot?, upsert?, remove? } guarded by watch id.

nodes.title: electron/main.ts
nodes.summary: Deepest dependency: IPC registration, recursive crawl, ignore parsing, chokidar watching. Spec target: extract an indexer + ignore-rules module.
details: Holds collectMarkdownFiles, collectWorkspaceIgnoreRules, isIgnoredByRules and the desktop:watch-path watcher.
TECH.md: move discovery/watching into fileIndex.ts, ignore logic into ignoreRules.ts, keep grants + IPC in main.

nodes.title: components/Sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Leaf view that renders workspace.files via the shared UI. Spec target: render an indexing state and allow currentPath to be absent.
details: Adapts files into @hubble.md/ui Sidebar and calls loadPath on select.
PRODUCT/TECH: show a compact indexing affordance and no selected row when the open file is not indexed.

tour.title: Start at the only changed files
tour.body: This PR adds just three docs. They are the entry point for understanding which code seams the change will eventually touch.

tour.title: Renderer entry point
tour.body: App.tsx is the highest-level seam: it owns the watcher effects and startup restoration the spec wants to rewire away from full re-lists.

tour.title: Store actions
tour.body: App depends on actions.ts for refreshFiles/openWorkspace/loadPath. The spec adds delta reducers here and decouples document loading from indexing.

tour.title: The IPC seam
tour.body: Both App and actions depend on the typed desktopApi bridge. A new index-subscription contract is the main interface the spec introduces.

tour.title: Deepest dependency
tour.body: The bridge invokes main.ts, the only disk owner. The spec extracts its crawl/ignore logic into fileIndex.ts and ignoreRules.ts.

tour.title: Leaf view
tour.body: Sidebar.tsx is the leaf: it consumes store state and calls actions. It gains the indexing state and absent-current-path tolerance.

graphs.label: User action graph
graphs.summary: What the user does when opening a large folder and how the specced behavior responds: the shell stays usable, the sidebar shows an indexing state, files open during indexing, and the list updates incrementally.

nodes.title: Open a folder
nodes.summary: The user opens a Workspace Folder or Plain Folder (picker, menu, or launch path).
details: openWorkspace sets workspacePath and clears files; PRODUCT behavior 1 requires entering the workspace immediately.
Folder open is local-first; no Workspace config or Cloud Sync is required (PRODUCT 26).

nodes.title: Editor shell stays usable
nodes.summary: The main editor shell remains interactive while the sidebar indexes; Open File and Open Folder stay available.
details: PRODUCT 1–3: the shell must not block on the initial sidebar crawl.
ADR-0008: the editor shell can become usable before the navigation index is ready.

nodes.title: Sidebar indexing state
nodes.summary: During the first crawl the sidebar shows an indexing/loading state, not a partial tree, then swaps to a complete tree.
details: PRODUCT 2 + 12: show indexing while crawling, then a complete markdown-only tree.
PRODUCT 13: expanding a folder is presentational and does not trigger a new crawl.

nodes.title: Open / restore a file during indexing
nodes.summary: The user opens a file explicitly, or Hubble restores the remembered last-opened file, even before the sidebar tree is ready.
details: PRODUCT 4–5: explicit picks and readable remembered files load while indexing continues.
PRODUCT 10: an ignored file stays viewable; ignore rules filter the sidebar, not file access.

nodes.title: Incremental sidebar updates
nodes.summary: As files change, the sidebar updates incrementally after a short debounce; unrelated non-markdown changes do not reload it.
details: PRODUCT 14–18: add/edit/delete markdown and ignore-file edits update visible results.
PRODUCT 19 + 23: non-markdown changes cause no visible reload; rapid changes settle into one update.

tour.title: User opens a folder
tour.body: The walkthrough starts with the triggering action: opening a (potentially large) folder, which today can hang the app.

tour.title: Shell stays usable
tour.body: The core behavioral promise: the editor shell stays interactive and Open File/Folder remain available while indexing runs.

tour.title: Sidebar shows indexing
tour.body: Instead of a partial or frozen tree, the sidebar shows a dedicated indexing state, then swaps to a complete markdown-only tree.

tour.title: Open a file mid-index
tour.body: The user can still open or restore a file during indexing — including ignored files — because the index filters the sidebar, not file access.

tour.title: Incremental updates
tour.body: Finally, file and ignore-rule changes update the sidebar incrementally after a debounce, while unrelated non-markdown churn causes no visible reload.
